ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers bottled/drinking water as the nearest defensible category; no brand-specific code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard small bottle of Olandia Water, approximately 350 ml, as typically sold at Filipino cafes and convenience outlets in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Water, tap, drinking — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174831)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Olandia Water is a branded bottled drinking water product from the Philippines. No brand-specific nutrient panel is available in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for plain drinking water (FDC ID 174831), which is the closest defensible approved-source match. All core macros are confirmed zero for plain water by nature. Mineral content (sodium, potassium, calcium, magnesium) is reported as trace or negligible in plain drinking water and set to confirmed values per USDA data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pure water contains no glycemic carbohydrates and therefore has no measurable glycemic index. By established nutritional convention, water is assigned GI = 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) and gi_category = 'low'. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0. This convention is consistent with peer-reviewed GI literature (Atkinson et al., 2021, AJCN) which excludes water from GI tables precisely because it elicits no glycemic response.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (approximately 4 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Olandia Water contains negligible sodium (~14 mg per 350 ml serving) and is entirely appropriate for individuals managing hypertension; adequate daily water intake supports blood pressure regulation and renal function.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Olandia Water contains no carbohydrates, no sugars, and has a glycemic load of zero per serving, making it the optimal beverage choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes who require strict glycemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, zero sodium of clinical significance
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Olandia Water contributes no dietary fat, cholesterol, or clinically significant sodium, and adequate hydration supports cardiovascular function; it is unreservedly suitable for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Olandia Water is a branded bottled drinking water product associated with Filipino food and beverage culture, sold at Filipino cafes and food outlets in Singapore such as Lucky Cafe on North Canal Road. As a plain purified drinking water, it contains zero calories, zero macronutrients (protein, fat, carbohydrate), and zero sugar. It provides only trace minerals naturally present in water, including small amounts of calcium, magnesium, potassium, and sodium at levels typical of purified drinking water. Olandia Water has no glycemic impact whatsoever, making it the ideal beverage for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. It is universally suitable across all dietary frameworks: vegetarian, vegan, halal, kosher, gluten-free, and dairy-free. In the context of Filipino dining in Singapore, bottled water such as Olandia is commonly ordered alongside meals featuring rich, savoury dishes such as adobo, sinigang, or pancit, serving as a neutral palate cleanser and hydration source. From a clinical nutrition standpoint, plain water remains the gold-standard beverage recommendation for all patient populations. The product is minimally processed (purified and bottled) and its nutritional profile is identical to that of plain drinking water as documented in USDA FoodData Central.
common_user_queries: How many calories does Olandia Water have?
Is Olandia Water suitable for diabetics?
Can I drink Olandia Water if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of Olandia Water?
Where can I buy Olandia Water in Singapore?
Is Olandia Water a Filipino brand?

confidence_reason: Olandia Water is a branded bottled water product. No brand-specific nutrient panel was found in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded Foods, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy plain drinking water data (FDC ID 174831), which is the closest defensible approved-source match. The nutritional profile of plain water is universally consistent regardless of brand, so the adaptation is scientifically sound, but confidence is set to 'medium' rather than 'high' because no exact brand-specific entry was located in an approved source.
notes: Olandia Water is a branded bottled drinking water product sold at Filipino food outlets in Singapore. All core macros (energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are naturally zero: energy_kcal is naturally zero as plain water contains no caloric nutrients; protein_g is naturally zero as water contains no protein by nature; fat_g is naturally zero as water contains no fat by nature; carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as water contains no carbohydrates by nature. Sugar_g, dietary_fibre_g, saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are all naturally zero for the same reason. Confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174831). Sodium_mg is set to 4.0 mg per 100 ml based on USDA data for plain drinking water; this is a trace amount of no clinical significance. Potassium_mg (1.0), calcium_mg (7.0), and magnesium_mg (2.0) reflect trace mineral content typical of purified drinking water per USDA SR Legacy. All vitamin fields are set to 0.0 as plain water contains no vitamins; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. Iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are set to 0.0 as confirmed zero for plain water per USDA FoodData Central. Water_g is 100.0 as this food is entirely water by composition. Glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. Glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0. No -1 sentinel values are used in this record; all fields have been resolved to confirmed values from the approved source. FoodOn match type is 'closest_parent' as no brand-specific FoodOn URI exists. FoodEx2 match type is 'closest_parent' as no brand-specific FoodEx2 code exists. SNOMED CT match type is 'closest_parent' as no brand-specific SNOMED code exists. Filipino cuisine multilingual naming applied: Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names provided. Romanized Filipino term 'Olandia Tubig' (tubig = water in Filipino/Tagalog) included as romanized_local and in aliases.
